metadata.title: The Loop Is the Product: Measuring Bounded Recursion as a System Primitive for Auditable AI
metadata.subtitle: An Empirical Companion to the Ouroboros Thesis
metadata.description:
This is the empirical companion to The Ouroboros Thesis (Lutar, 2026; Zenodo: 10.5281/zenodo.19867281). The v1 paper was a position and systems-design preprint and made no empirical claims. v2 reports a shipped reference implementation of the four v1 primitives as a TypeScript runtime (@szl-holdings/ouroboros, 142/142 tests passing, deterministic replay, opt-in adaptive depth with bit-identical invariant), three production case studies on the same kernel (A11oy, Sentra, Amaru) with shared trace persistence via aef-evidence-ledger, and closure of the five-item experimental agenda from v1 §9, including a new loop-budget Pareto frontier and a pre-registered small-N crossover study on whether convergence traces change human audit behavior.

The paper includes a falsification ledger (§3.7) listing nine load-bearing claims paired with the specific observations that would refute them, and a reproducibility manifest (§10) pinning runtime, thesis, and platform commits along with file blob SHAs. Cross-system distillation is deliberately held as a hypothesis, not promoted to a result.

Central claim defended: when convergence is measurable, the trace is not a log but a deliverable — one that downstream audit, policy compilation, and regulated procurement can consume as evidence rather than as documentation.

Mapped to NIST AI RMF MEASURE/MANAGE, EU AI Act Article 12 record-keeping, and the 2026-04-30 NYSTEC government-readiness audit (A11oy 72/100, Sentra 68/100, Amaru 65/100).
references: Dehghani et al., 2018. Universal Transformers. ICLR 2019.
Banino et al., 2021. PonderNet: Learning to Ponder. ICML.
Graves, 2016. Adaptive Computation Time for Recurrent Neural Networks. arXiv:1603.08983.
Lutar, S.P., 2026. The Ouroboros Thesis: Looped Computation as a System Primitive for AI Systems. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19867281
metadata.notes: Companion paper to Zenodo 19867281 (v1 position paper). v2 closes the v1 §9 experimental agenda with shipped, version-pinned implementations and pre-registered analyses. Falsification ledger (§3.7) and reproducibility manifest (§10) included. Replication harness is MIT-licensed; the runtime is proprietary.
